Chair members of the committee so Senate bill 273 is essentially a package that was developed by the Senate Finance Committee in response to House Bill mine we had about three hearings where we brought in leaders local leaders from Torrance Otero and Sebela County asked them for data asked them for the costs and projections of the potential impact from everything from property taxes the GRT loss and the various financial hardships that they would face as a result of House Bill 9 so what was prepared in Senate Finance was Senate bill 273 which basically provided them with funding for the next few years and then for Otero for one year for Otero it was designed specifically to help pay the bond payments that are due for the prison facility that they still are in debt for and then for currently that facility also has prisoners from Hemis and other places around the area and then for Torrance it was focused on Estancia and then for the county as well and with both of those it was more tailored towards water needs in that area to help them address that gap that they had to be able to then bridge and build off economic development so that was that year it included language that in the event that any of those prisons ended up getting another contract and then being functional the payments from the state would stop right so it was very much a decoupling provision and this was supposed to serve as kind of a a bridge so this way during the interim the legislature can really dive in and really help out these communities build out their economic development areas with Senate bill 274 that is the governor's grow and the governor's gap with NCYFD so that bill focused on four different things it included money for aging long-term services for New Mexico care it included
